Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 8 percent driven by stronger-than-expected iPhone demand in the back-to-school season and continued growth in Services revenue, which reached a new all-time quarterly record, partially offset by a decline in Mac and iPad.

iPhone revenue accelerated to 7 percent growth year-over-year as the new product cycle commenced and carrier trade-in promotions drove elevated activity, reversing the competitive intensity headwinds in China from prior periods.

Gross margin expanded meaningfully as the new iPhone product mix and pricing discipline combined to more than offset increases in manufacturing and freight costs, accelerating above the stable levels of prior quarters.

Capital expenditures increased year-over-year as we invested in advanced manufacturing equipment and silicon design tooling to support our custom chip roadmap for the next several years.

Item 3. Other Information
